title: HeadObject, GetObject, Conditions, Ranges, and Cleanup · Execution Trace: Download Error Boundary
summary: A download service calls GET to discover metadata, leaks response bodies, retries after sending headers, and returns full private objects for malformed ranges. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the download error boundary increment in bounded S3 download decision and cleanup flow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify missing key, missing version, delete marker, denied access, archive restore state, throttling, cancellation, and partial downstream response.
Validate one bounded range, map inclusive offsets, cap response bytes, preserve Content-Range, and reject unsatisfiable or abusive requests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Download Error Boundary · Theory Download Error Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 9286 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove S3 response-body cleanup; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify missing key, missing version, delete marker, denied access, archive restore state, throttling, cancellation, and partial downstream response.
